evidence: model package hosts standalone records/classes: AdjectiveProfile, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, Edge, ExploreOptions, ExplorationResult, ComparisonResult — but WordSketchResult, CollocateResult, KwicResult, SnippetResult are nested inside QueryResults in the query package, splitting domain value objects across two homes with no documented rationale

ExploreParams (private record at line 382 of ExplorationHandlers.java) near-duplicates ExploreOptions (public record in model package): both bundle topCollocates, minShared, minLogDice, and a per-seed noun limit; they diverge only on one field (includeExamples vs nounsPerSeed)

API handler classes (ExplorationHandlers, SketchHandlers, RelationUtils) are all package-private, while HttpApiUtils is public — a subtle access-modifier inconsistency among siblings in the same api package

ComparisonResult and ExplorationResult carry explicit Javadoc documenting the record-vs-class split as intentional; no equivalent rationale exists for why result types in QueryResults are nested rather than standalone

strengths: Strict camelCase naming throughout all packages with zero snake_case drift — no naming convention collisions across the codebase
The intentional record-vs-class distinction in the model package is explicitly documented in ComparisonResult and ExplorationResult Javadoc, preventing false-positive confusion
All API handler siblings (ExplorationHandlers, SketchHandlers, RelationUtils) follow the same structural pattern: package-private class, private final fields set via constructor, no public state

Private nested records for implementation details (HitRecord, CollocateSearch in BlackLabQueryExecutor; ExploreParams in ExplorationHandlers) are consistently used as local value bundles without leaking to the API surface
convention_outlier.issue_character: The convention issues are localized to two closely related phenomena: a split in where domain data types live (model package vs nested-in-QueryResults), and a partial duplication of an explore-parameters bundle record, suggesting the model package boundary was not revisited when QueryResults was introduced.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The codebase is clean and consistent in naming, access modifiers, and structural patterns, placing it well above a 60 (significant drag) anchor. The main drag comes from a tangible split in where domain value objects live — result types in query.QueryResults vs the model package — which creates a navigation inconsistency developers will notice when adding new result types. The near-duplicate ExploreParams/ExploreOptions adds a second, smaller drift point. Together these pull the score below 80 (solid but uneven).

issues.summary: Domain result records live in query.QueryResults instead of the model package, splitting data-type ownership across two homes
evidence: WordSketchResult, CollocateResult, KwicResult, SnippetResult are nested records inside QueryResults in the query package
AdjectiveProfile, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, Edge, ExploreOptions are standalone public records in the model package
No Javadoc or comment explains why query-layer results are co-located in QueryResults rather than placed in model like their siblings
issues.suggestion: Move WordSketchResult, CollocateResult, KwicResult, and SnippetResult (and the ConcordanceResult sealed interface) to the model package as standalone top-level types, matching the convention established by AdjectiveProfile and DiscoveredNoun. Retain QueryResults only as a thin utility class or delete it.

issues.summary: ExploreParams (private record in ExplorationHandlers) near-duplicates ExploreOptions (public record in model), both bundling the same exploration tuning parameters
evidence: ExploreParams at line 382 of ExplorationHandlers: record ExploreParams(int topCollocates, int minShared, double minLogDice, int nounsPerSeed)
ExploreOptions in model package: record ExploreOptions(int topCollocates, int nounsPerCollocate, double minLogDice, int minShared, boolean includeExamples)
Four of the five fields overlap; the handler converts ExploreParams into ExploreOptions before calling SemanticFieldExplorer
issues.suggestion: Eliminate ExploreParams by having parseExploreParams return an ExploreOptions directly. Reconcile the one divergent field (nounsPerSeed vs nounsPerCollocate) in ExploreOptions and add the includeExamples default inline at the call site.

root_causes: QueryResults was created as a convenience namespace before the model package was established as the canonical home for domain value types; the boundary was never realigned
ExploreParams was added as a quick HTTP-parsing local bundle without checking that ExploreOptions in model already served the same purpose
